summary: Bandit security scan completed on 258 Python files (111,720 LOC) across suite-core/core/, suite-api/apps/, and suite-attack/api/. No HIGH severity findings detected. 53 MEDIUM and 155 LOW severity issues identified, primarily B608 (SQL injection, 26), B105 (hardcoded SQL, 24), B110 (try/except/pass, 82), and B311 (pickle usage, 15). Overall security posture is GOOD with no critical vulnerabilities requiring immediate action.
issues: MEDIUM (53 findings): SQL injection patterns (B608, B105), hardcoded credentials (B104), unsafe tempfile handling (B108), XML parsing of untrusted input (B314), pickle deserialization (B311)
LOW (155 findings): Code quality issues (B110 try/except/pass dominant, 82 occurrences), subprocess calls (B603, B607), URL scheme validation (B310)

top_finding_types.description: Possible SQL injection via string-based query
Probable use of pickle, marshal, etc.
Audit url open for permitted schemes

scan_details.critical_findings: None - no HIGH severity issues found
scan_details.recommendation: Address SQL injection patterns (B608, B105) by parameterizing queries; review pickle usage (B311) for deserialization attacks; audit hardcoded IPs (B104)
